contents.value:
```typescript
@Inject(token: any)
```
Specifies a custom provider via an injection token.

```typescript
constructor(@Inject(API_URL) private apiUrl: string) {}
```

```typescript
@Optional()
```
Marks a dependency as optional. The injector provides `null` if the dependency is not found.

```typescript
constructor(@Optional() private logger?: LoggerService) {}
```

```typescript
@Self()
```
Instructs the DI framework to resolve a dependency only from the local injector.

```typescript
@SkipSelf()
```
Instructs the DI framework to resolve a dependency starting from the parent injector.

```typescript
@Host()
```
Instructs the DI framework to resolve a dependency from the host element's injector.

```typescript
function computed<T>(computation: () => T): Signal<T>
```
Creates a memoized signal that derives its value from other signals. Re-evaluates only when dependencies change.

```typescript
const count = signal(2);
const doubled = computed(() => count() * 2);
console.log(doubled()); // 4
```

```typescript
function input<T>(initialValue?: T): InputSignal<T>
```
Signal-based input property. Alternative to `@Input` decorator with signal semantics.

```typescript
name = input<string>();          // optional
id = input.required<number>();   // required
alias = input('default');        // with default
```

```typescript
function output<T>(): OutputEmitterRef<T>
```
Signal-based output property. Alternative to `@Output` decorator.

```typescript
clicked = output<void>();
this.clicked.emit();
```

```typescript
function model<T>(initialValue?: T): ModelSignal<T>
```
Creates a model signal for two-way data binding. Combines input and output in a single API.

```typescript
value = model<string>('');  // parent uses [(value)]
```

```typescript
function viewChild<T>(locator: string | Type<T>): Signal<T | undefined>
```
Signal-based view child query. Returns a signal that updates when the queried element changes.

```typescript
myInput = viewChild<ElementRef>('myInput');
myInputRequired = viewChild.required<ElementRef>('myInput');
```

```typescript
function viewChildren<T>(locator: string | Type<T>): Signal<ReadonlyArray<T>>
```
Signal-based view children query. Returns a signal with all matching elements.

```typescript
function contentChild<T>(locator: string | Type<T>): Signal<T | undefined>
```
Signal-based content child query. Queries projected content for a single matching element.

```typescript
function contentChildren<T>(locator: string | Type<T>): Signal<ReadonlyArray<T>>
```
Signal-based content children query. Queries all matching projected content elements.

```typescript
function linkedSignal<T>(computation: () => T): WritableSignal<T>
```
Creates a writable signal that resets to a computed value whenever a source signal changes.

```typescript
const selected = linkedSignal(() => this.items()[0]);
```

```typescript
function toSignal<T>(source: Observable<T>, options?: ToSignalOptions<T>): Signal<T>
```
Converts an Observable to a signal. Values emitted by the Observable update the signal.

```typescript
const data = toSignal(this.data$, { initialValue: [] });
```

```typescript
function toObservable<T>(source: Signal<T>): Observable<T>
```
Converts a signal to an Observable that emits whenever the signal value changes.

```typescript
enum ViewEncapsulation {
  Emulated = 0,
  None = 2,
  ShadowDom = 3
}
```
Defines template and style encapsulation options.

- **Emulated** (default): Scoped styles via attribute selectors
- **None**: Global styles, no encapsulation
- **ShadowDom**: Native Shadow DOM

```typescript
abstract class ChangeDetectorRef
```
Provides change detection methods: `detectChanges()`, `markForCheck()`, `detach()`, `reattach()`.

```typescript
constructor(private cdr: ChangeDetectorRef) {}
this.cdr.markForCheck();
```

```typescript
abstract class TemplateRef<C>
```
Represents an embedded template that can be used to instantiate embedded views.

```typescript
abstract class ViewContainerRef
```
Container where views can be attached. Provides `createComponent()`, `createEmbeddedView()`, `insert()`, `remove()`, etc.

```typescript
abstract class Renderer2
```
Platform-agnostic DOM rendering abstraction. Methods include `createElement()`, `setAttribute()`, `setStyle()`, `listen()`.

```typescript
class FormControl<T = any> extends AbstractControl
```
Tracks the value and validation status of an individual form control.

```typescript
const name = new FormControl('', Validators.required);
console.log(name.value);  // ''
console.log(name.valid);  // false
```

```typescript
class FormArray extends AbstractControl
```
Tracks the value and validity of an array of form controls.

```typescript
const arr = new FormArray([
  new FormControl('item1'),
  new FormControl('item2')
]);
```

```typescript
class Validators
```
Built-in validators: `required`, `minLength(n)`, `maxLength(n)`, `pattern(regex)`, `email`, `min(n)`, `max(n)`, `requiredTrue`, `compose()`, `composeAsync()`.

```typescript
class Router
```
Service for navigation among views. Key methods: `navigate()`, `navigateByUrl()`, `createUrlTree()`.

```typescript
constructor(private router: Router) {}
this.router.navigate(['/dashboard']);
```

```typescript
class ActivatedRoute
```
Provides access to route information: `params`, `queryParams`, `data`, `paramMap`, `snapshot`, `url`.

```typescript
constructor(private route: ActivatedRoute) {}
this.route.params.subscribe(p => console.log(p['id']));
```

```typescript
<router-outlet></router-outlet>
```
Placeholder directive that Angular dynamically fills with the component matching the current route.

```typescript
class HttpClient
```
Injectable service for HTTP requests. Methods: `get()`, `post()`, `put()`, `delete()`, `patch()`, `request()`.

```typescript
this.http.get<User[]>('/api/users').subscribe(users => { });
```

```typescript
function provideHttpClient(...features: HttpFeature[]): EnvironmentProviders
```
Sets up HttpClient providers for standalone apps.

```typescript
provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([authInterceptor]), withFetch())
```

```typescript
function bootstrapApplication(rootComponent: Type<unknown>, options?: ApplicationConfig): Promise<ApplicationRef>
```
Bootstraps a standalone Angular application.

```typescript
bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, appConfig)
  .catch(err => console.error(err));
```

```html
...
...
``` Structural directive that conditionally includes a template. Consider using `@if` for new projects.

```html
  • {{ item.name }}
    • ``` Structural directive that renders a template for each item. Consider using `@for` for new projects.

```html
{{ observable$ | async }}
```
Subscribes to an Observable or Promise and returns the latest value. Automatically unsubscribes on destroy.

```typescript
function inject<T>(token: ProviderToken<T>, options?: InjectOptions): T
```
Injects a dependency from the current injector context. Must be called in an injection context (constructor, factory, etc.).

```typescript
const http = inject(HttpClient);
const router = inject(Router);
```

```typescript
function takeUntilDestroyed(destroyRef?: DestroyRef): MonoTypeOperatorFunction<T>
```
RxJS operator that completes an Observable when the component/service is destroyed.

```typescript
this.data$.pipe(takeUntilDestroyed()).subscribe(...);
```

```typescript
abstract class DestroyRef
```
Allows registering cleanup callbacks.

```typescript
const destroyRef = inject(DestroyRef);
destroyRef.onDestroy(() => cleanup());
```

```typescript
abstract class Injector
```
Retrieves instances of dependencies by token. Can be used to manually resolve dependencies.

```typescript
class InjectionToken<T>
```
Creates a DI token for cases where the type alone is insufficient to identify a dependency.

```typescript
export const API_URL = new InjectionToken<string>('API_URL');
```

```typescript
class RouterModule
```
Adds router directives and providers. Use `RouterModule.forRoot(routes)` in the root module and `RouterModule.forChild(routes)` in feature modules.
```html Link ``` Directive that turns user clicks into router navigations. Can be applied to any element.
```html Home ``` Tracks whether the linked route is currently active and sets CSS classes.

```typescript
class ReactiveFormsModule
```
Provides directives for reactive forms: `formControl`, `formGroup`, `formArrayName`, `formControlName`.

```typescript
class FormsModule
```
Provides `ngModel` and related directives for template-driven forms.

```typescript
class CommonModule
```
Exports all the basic Angular directives and pipes: NgIf, NgFor, NgClass, NgStyle, DatePipe, AsyncPipe, etc.

```html
...
``` Adds and removes CSS classes based on an expression.

```html
...
``` Updates inline styles based on an expression.

```html

A

Default

``` Switches templates based on matching values.

```html
{{ amount | currency:'USD':'symbol':'1.2-2' }}
```
Transforms a number to a currency string according to locale rules.

```typescript
function withInterceptors(interceptors: HttpInterceptorFn[]): HttpFeature
```
Configures functional HTTP interceptors.

```typescript
provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([authInterceptor, loggingInterceptor]))
```

```typescript
function withFetch(): HttpFeature
```
Configures HttpClient to use the Fetch API instead of XMLHttpRequest.

```typescript
function withComponentInputBinding(): RouterFeature
```
Binds route parameters directly to component inputs.

```typescript
provideRouter(routes, withComponentInputBinding())
```

```typescript
function importProvidersFrom(...sources: ImportProvidersSource[]): EnvironmentProviders
```
Collects providers from NgModules for standalone configuration.

```typescript
importProvidersFrom(BrowserAnimationsModule)
```

```typescript
function provideZoneChangeDetection(options?: { eventCoalescing?: boolean }): EnvironmentProviders
```
Provides NgZone-based change detection with optional event coalescing.

```typescript
class HttpHeaders
```
Immutable HTTP headers. Create with `new HttpHeaders({ 'Content-Type': 'application/json' })`.

```typescript
class HttpParams
```
Immutable URL parameters. Chain with `.set()`, `.append()`, `.delete()`.

```typescript
new HttpParams().set('page', '1').set('limit', '10')
```
